mappings.rationaleAI: Mesopotamian civilizations cover river valley development (B.1), Sumerian city-states (B.1.b), Sumer and Akkad (B.1.c), Old Babylonian period (B.1.d), Syria (D.1), and Hittite conflicts (E.2).
Ancient Egypt addresses river valley civilization (C) and the New Kingdom (E.1).
Egyptian art relates to Egyptian civilization (C) and New Kingdom culture (E.1.c).
Egyptian mythology relates to Egyptian civilization (C) and New Kingdom (E.1).
Hieroglyphs relate to Egyptian civilization (C) and New Kingdom culture (E.1.c).
Babylonia addresses Old Babylonian Period (B.1.d), Mesopotamia c. 1600-900 BC (E.3), and Neo-Babylonian Empire (G.2.a).
Assyria addresses early Assyria (B.1.d.ii), rise of Assyria (E.3.c), and Assyrian Empire (G.1).
The Trojan War relates to Hittite Empire (E.2) and Mycenaean civilization (E.5).
Writing history relates to ancient Near Eastern civilizations (A) and cuneiform invention (B.1.b.i).
Biblical archaeology relates to Hebrew origins (E.2.b.ii) and the Hebrew kingdom (F.2.b).
Hebrew Bible literature relates to Yahweh cult and biblical literature (F.2.b.ii).
Judaism relates to Hebrew origins (E.2.b.ii) and Hebrew kingdom (F.2.b).
Iran relates to Achaemenid Empire (G.3) and Parthian/Sasanian empires (H).
Alexander relates to the conquest of Persia (G.3.b).
Hellenistic world relates to Seleucid rule (G.3.d).
Ancient warfare relates to Hittite conflicts (E.2) and Assyrian expansion (G.1).
Silk Road relates to Achaemenid territory (G.3).
Archaeology relates to archaeological sources (A).
African history relates to Nilotic Sudan and Ethiopia (I).
Late Antiquity relates to Sasanian Empire's end (H.2.c).
The Old Testament relates to Hebrew origins (E.2.b.ii) and Hebrew kingdom (F.2.b).
Dead Sea Scrolls relate to later Jewish history (F.2.b).
Hinduism's Indus Valley origins tangentially relate to Near Eastern civilization context (D.1).

Covers ancient Greek civilization from 1400 BCE through the Hellenistic period including interactions with Phoenicians and Alexander the Great, relevant to early Aegean civilizations and Mycenaean Greece.
Covers the Hebrew Bible including its composition and historical context, relevant to the origins of the Hebrews, the Exodus, and the Hebrew kingdom's cult of Yahweh and biblical literature.
Covers the rise of Islam and Arab conquests, relevant to the Sasanian Empire's decline and extinction under Arab conquest (636/637-651).
Covers the Holocene epoch, invention of agriculture, and development of cities, relevant to Stone Age cultures and Neolithic farming communities in Syria, Palestine, and Anatolia.
